Defendant Sunrun, Inc.'s Motion To Stay Action Pending Appellate Ruling is GRANTED.

Defendant Sunrun's unopposed request for judicial notice is granted.

(see word minutes for full details)

Defendants are ordered to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the above and email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the time set for hearing.

MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION
Off Calendar Aug-19-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Shounak Dharap, Esq. 415-495-7800 650-575-6485 ssd@arnslaw.com Arns Davis Law for Plaintiff.
Kristen Nesbit, Esq. 213-402-9568 Fisher & Phillips LLP for Defendant Sunrun.
Allison Weiss, Esq. 213-486-0048 Quintairos Prieto Wood & Boyer PA for Defendant Francisco Bandeira Jr.
Court reporter: Jane Hong, CSR #11975, 909-831-2644, csrjane@hotmail.com

ARGUED. Counsel for Sunrun Ms. Nesbit disputes the court's tentative ruling. Ms. Nesbit presents arguments on the allegations of sexual harrassment and battery. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

1 - DEFENDANT SUNRUN, INC.'s MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION.

Defendant Sunrun, Inc.'s motion to compel arbitration of and stay or dismiss the claims of plaintiff Evelyn Barrera is denied.

(see word minutes for full details).

(as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

Order is signed.

Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court reporter: Jane Hong, CSR #11975, 909-831-2644, csrjane@hotmail.com, Reported. (302/HEK)

MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION
Off Calendar Aug-19-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Shounak Dharap, Esq. 415-495-7800 650-575-6485 ssd@arnslaw.com Arns Davis Law for Plaintiff.
Kristen Nesbit, Esq. 213-402-9568 Fisher & Phillips LLP for Defendant Sunrun.
Allison Weiss, Esq. 213-486-0048 Quintairos Prieto Wood & Boyer PA for Defendant Francisco Bandeira Jr.
Court reporter: Jane Hong, CSR #11975, 909-831-2644, csrjane@hotmail.com

ARGUED. Counsel for Sunrun Ms. Nesbit disputes the court's tentative ruling. Ms. Nesbit presents arguments on the allegations of sexual harrassment and battery. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

2 - DEFENDANT FRANCISCO BANDEIRA's MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION.

Defendant Francisco Bandeira's joinder in defendant Sunrun, Inc.'s motion to compel arbitration of and stay or dismiss the claims of plaintiff Evelyn Barrera is denied for the reasons stated in the ruling denying the motion sought to be joined.

Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court reporter: Jane Hong, CSR #11975, 909-831-2644, csrjane@hotmail.com, Reported. (302/HEK)
